scope: Operational recovery note for the future append-only staging reconciliation migration. It authorizes no database change by itself.
trigger: Any non-zero migration command, database SQLSTATE/error, unexpected schema result, missing RPC after deployment, or failed atomic smoke check.
immediateActions: Keep pilot exposure paused; do not create real or additional test transactions.
Stop the migration command at the first failure. Do not retry with modified SQL in the console.
Preserve the raw command output, PostgreSQL SQLSTATE, error message, failing migration version, UTC time, and deployed Git commit.
Capture read-only after-state evidence: migration ledger row status, target RPC resolution result, and affected object existence checks.
Record whether the database transaction rolled back or the migration completed before a later smoke check failed.
prohibitedActions: Do not run Supabase migration repair, insert/update/delete migration-ledger rows, or mark the version applied manually.
Do not manually insert, update, delete, or backfill production-like leads, offers, transactions, participants, documents, workflow lanes, or notifications to make the check pass.
Do not reset, restore over, truncate, or recreate staging as an incident response.
Do not edit, delete, rename, or re-date historical migration files.
Do not expose the pilot while failure evidence is unresolved.

preflightOrDdlFailure.expectedDatabaseState: The reconciliation migration is transactional. A failure before commit should leave no partial DDL from that migration.
preflightOrDdlFailure.recovery: Verify the rollback with read-only schema/ledger probes, diagnose the exact failed precondition, and prepare a new forward migration or corrected preflight before another attempt.

postCommitContractOrSmokeFailure.expectedDatabaseState: The new migration version may be recorded and its additive schema may exist; do not attempt to undo it manually.
postCommitContractOrSmokeFailure.recovery: Leave the schema in place, preserve smoke evidence, and prepare one new append-only correction migration. Use only clearly labelled synthetic test data for any subsequent verification.

unexpectedBusinessDataObservation.expectedDatabaseState: No operator should modify the observed record during triage.
unexpectedBusinessDataObservation.recovery: Stop exposure, preserve identifiers in the restricted incident record, and decide whether a separately reviewed forward data-repair migration is required.

minimumIncidentRecord: migration filename, version, SHA-256, and Git commit
raw command output and database SQLSTATE/error text, redacted for secrets and unnecessary personal data
decision: remain paused, forward correction proposed, or verified recovery
resumeCriteria: A reviewed forward correction or verified retry has completed successfully.
The linked ledger has a comprehensible append-only history with no manual repair.
The named RPC resolves and the full post-deploy contract smoke checks pass.
The incident record is complete and pilot exposure is explicitly re-approved.
